nodes.title: Uniqueness of the Maximum
nodes.label: prop:uniqueness-of-the-maximum
nodes.text_preview: [Uniqueness of the Maximum] Let $A $. If $m_1$ and $m_2$ are both maximum elements of $A$, then $m_1=m_2$.

nodes.title: Uniqueness of the Supremum
nodes.label: prop:uniqueness-of-the-supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Uniqueness of the Supremum] Let $A $. If $s_1$ and $s_2$ are both suprema of $A$, then $s_1=s_2$.

nodes.title: Translation Invariance of the Supremum
nodes.label: thm:translation-invariance-supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Translation Invariance of the Supremum] Let $A $ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $c $. Then \[ (A+c)= A+c, A+c=\ a+c:a A\ . \]

nodes.label: thm:scalar-mult-positive

proof_file_blocks.title: Scalar Multiplication by a Positive Scalar

nodes.text_preview: [Scalar Multiplication by a Positive Scalar] Let $A $ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $k>0$. Then \[ (kA)=k A, kA=\ ka:a A\ . \]

nodes.label: thm:scalar-mult-negative

proof_file_blocks.title: Scalar Multiplication by a Negative Scalar

nodes.text_preview: [Scalar Multiplication by a Negative Scalar] Let $A $ be nonempty and bounded below, and let $k<0$. Then \[ (kA)=k A, kA=\ ka:a A\ . \]

nodes.title: Negation Exchanges Supremum and Infimum
nodes.label: thm:negation-exchange-sup-inf

proof_file_blocks.title: Strategy: The Choice of Leastness Method

nodes.text_preview: [Negation Exchanges Supremum and Infimum] Let $A $ be nonempty and bounded below. Then $-A=\ -a:a A\ $ is bounded above and \[ (-A)=- A. \]

nodes.title: Supremum of a Sum Set
nodes.label: thm:supremum-sum-set
nodes.text_preview: [Supremum of a Sum Set] Let $A,B $ be nonempty and bounded above. Then \[ (A+B)= A+ B, A+B=\ a+b:a A,\ b B\ . \]

nodes.title: Supremum of a Difference Set
nodes.label: thm:supremum-difference-set
nodes.text_preview: [Supremum of a Difference Set] Let $A,B $ be nonempty, with $A$ bounded above and $B$ bounded below. Then \[ (A-B)= A- B, A-B=\ a-b:a A,\ b B\ . \]

nodes.label: thm:supremum-absolute-value-image
nodes.text_preview: [Supremum of the Absolute-Value Image] Let $A $ be nonempty and bounded. Then \[ |A|= \ | A|,| A|\ , |A|=\ |a|:a A\ . \]

nodes.title: Bounds for Suprema and Infima Under Set Addition
nodes.label: prop:bounds-sum-set

proof_file_blocks.title: Bounds for Suprema and Infima Under Set Addition

nodes.text_preview: [Bounds for Suprema and Infima Under Set Addition] Let $A,B $ be nonempty and bounded. Then \[ A+ B (A+B) (A+B) A+ B. \]

nodes.title: Upper Bounds Depend on the Ambient Order
nodes.label: prop:upper-bounds-ambient-order

proof_file_blocks.title: Upper Bounds Depend on the Ambient Order

nodes.text_preview: [Upper Bounds Depend on the Ambient Order] Let $(S, _S)$ and $(T, _T)$ be ordered sets, and let $A S T$. Whether $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ must be interpreted relative to the chosen ambient order.

nodes.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set
nodes.label: prop:suprema-ambient-set

proof_file_blocks.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set

nodes.text_preview: [Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set] Let $A S S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.

nodes.title: Ambient Existence of Supremum
nodes.label: thm:ambient-existence-supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Ambient Existence of Supremum] There are ordered sets $S S'$ and a subset $A S$ such that $A$ has a supremum relative to $S'$ but has no supremum relative to $S$.

nodes.title: Rational Gap Example for Suprema
nodes.label: lem:rational-gap-suprema
nodes.text_preview: [Rational Gap Example for Suprema] Let \[ A=\ q :q^2<2\ . \] Then $A$ is nonempty and bounded above in $ $, but $A$ has no supremum relative to $ $.

nodes.title: Epsilon Characterization of Supremum
nodes.label: def:epsilon-characterization-of-supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Epsilon Characterization of Supremum] Let $A $ and let $s $. The element $s$ satisfies the of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and for every $ >0$ there exists $a A$ such that $s- <a$.

nodes.title: Epsilon Characterization of Infimum
nodes.label: def:epsilon-characterization-of-infimum
nodes.text_preview: [Epsilon Characterization of Infimum] Let $A $ and let $t $. The element $t$ satisfies the of $A$ if and only if $t$ is a lower bound of $A$ and for every $ >0$ there exists $a A$ such that $a<t+ $.

nodes.title: Upper Bound
nodes.label: def:upper-bound
nodes.text_preview: [Upper Bound] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set, let $A S$, and let $u S$. The element $u$ is an of $A$ if and only if $x u$ for every $x A$.

nodes.title: Lower Bound
nodes.label: def:lower-bound
nodes.text_preview: [Lower Bound] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set, $A S$, and $l S$. The element $l$ is a of $A$ if and only if every $x A$ satisfies $l x$.

nodes.title: Minimum
nodes.label: def:minimum
nodes.text_preview: [Minimum] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set, $A S$, and $m A$. The element $m$ is the of $A$ if and only if for every $x A$ one has $m x$.

nodes.title: Maximum
nodes.label: def:maximum
nodes.text_preview: [Maximum] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set and let $A S$. An element $M S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M A$ and every $x A$ satisfies $x M$.

nodes.title: Supremum
nodes.label: def:supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Supremum] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set, let $A S$, and let $s S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s u$.

nodes.title: Infimum
nodes.label: def:infimum
nodes.text_preview: [Infimum] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set, let $A S$, and let $i S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l i$.

nodes.title: Least Upper Bound Property Implies Existence of Suprema
nodes.label: thm:lub-property-implies-existence-of-suprema

proof_file_blocks.title: Least Upper Bound Property Implies Existence of Suprema

nodes.text_preview: [Least Upper Bound Property Implies Existence of Suprema] Let $S$ be an ordered set with the least upper bound property. If $A S$ is nonempty and bounded above in $S$, then $A$ has a supremum in $S$.

nodes.title: Upper Bound Property of Supremum
nodes.label: prop:upper-bound-property-of-supremum
nodes.text_preview: [Upper Bound Property of Supremum] Let $A $ and let $s= A$. Then $x s$ for every $x A$.

nodes.title: Archimedean Property
nodes.label: thm:archimedean-property
nodes.text_preview: [Archimedean Property] For every real number $x$, there exists a natural number $n$ such that $n>x$.

nodes.title: Archimedean Reciprocal Corollary
nodes.label: cor:archimedean-reciprocal

proof_file_blocks.title: Strategy: The Choice of Leastness Method

nodes.text_preview: [Archimedean Reciprocal Corollary] For every $x>0$ and every $y $, there exists $n $ such that $nx>y$.

nodes.title: Least Upper Bound Property
nodes.label: def:least-upper-bound-property
nodes.text_preview: [Least Upper Bound Property] Let $(S, )$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

nodes.title: Completeness of $ $
nodes.label: ax:completeness-of-reals
nodes.text_preview: [Completeness of $ $] The ordered field $ $ has the Least Upper Bound Property.

nodes.title: Dense Subset
nodes.label: def:dense-subset
nodes.text_preview: [Dense Subset] Let $D S $. The set $D$ is $S$ if and only if for every $x S$ and every $ >0$ there exists $d D$ such that $|x-d|< $.

nodes.title: Order Dense Subset
nodes.label: def:order-dense-subset
nodes.text_preview: [Order Dense Subset] Let $X$ be a linearly ordered set and let $A X$. The subset $A$ is $X$ if and only if for every $a,b X$ with $a<b$ there exists $c A$ such that $a<c<b$.

nodes.title: Density of the Rational Numbers in $ $
nodes.label: thm:density-of-rationals-in-reals

proof_file_blocks.title: Density of the Rational Numbers in $\mathbb{R}$

nodes.text_preview: [Density of the Rational Numbers in $ $] For every $a,b $ with $a<b$, there exists $q $ such that $a<q<b$.

nodes.title: Density of the Irrational Numbers in $ $
nodes.label: thm:density-of-irrationals-in-reals

proof_file_blocks.title: Density of the Irrational Numbers in $\mathbb{R}$

nodes.text_preview: [Density of the Irrational Numbers in $ $] For every $a,b $ with $a<b$, there exists $s $ such that $a<s<b$.

nodes.title: Rational and Irrational Points in Every Open Interval
nodes.label: cor:every-open-interval-contains-rational-and-irrational

proof_file_blocks.title: Rational and Irrational Points in Every Open Interval

nodes.text_preview: [Rational and Irrational Points in Every Open Interval] Every open interval in $ $ contains both a rational number and an irrational number.

nodes.title: Nested Interval Property
nodes.label: thm:nested-interval-theorem
nodes.text_preview: [Nested Interval Property] Let $(I_n)$ be a sequence of nonempty closed intervals in $ $. If $I_ n+1 I_n$ for every $n $, then \[ _ n=1 ^ I_n . \]
